notes: same as original game - apply virtual desktop of 640x480, set your screen resolution in game config, everything should work in window.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: To fix the problems with the menu: run the command WINEPREFIX=~/steam/steamapps/compatdata/32390/pfx/ winecfg, go to the Graphics section, enable Emulate a vrtual desktop and set the resolution to the real resolution of your normal screen (e.g. 1920 x 1080). If you have Problems with the mouse, disable Enable Free look in the ingame Setup at Controls in the Options section.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Game starts in a small area of the screen in the topleft corner. Keyboard input doesn't work which means you can't progress. Can't minimize or tab out either so you need to ALT+F4 out
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Game runs in the corner of my screen but cannot even click on menus properly.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Game seems to start ok but you UI doesn't work. Can't type or click anything. Can't alt+tab or alt+F4 or switch desktop
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes:
Game starts but menu doesnt render correctly; you can hear sound effects by moving your mouse over buttons and clicking them but the screen doesn't refresh making it impossible to progress.

Can't alt+tab, alt+f4 or switch desktop but by using escape and enter you can exit the game.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Game works out of the box on Proton with WineD3D but you have to enable in the game 3D acceleration, otherwise it is extremely slow. I copied manually dgVoodoo2, run protontricks GAMEID winecfg, set ddraw to native and then the game worked on Vulkan with DXVK.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Game just works out of the box now, no tweaking required. Select "hardware acceleration" in in-game display settings - after that more available resolutions will show up. It has a bit weird control scheme by default, but that's normal for such old game.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™

notes: Runs perfectly actually, even better than the original Jedi Knight (no menu issues). Just finished it. I turned 3D acceleration on in-game.
title: STAR WARS™ Jedi Knight - Mysteries of the Sith™
